meaning: adj.未受到照料的,被忽略了的
examples: An untended lawn or garden can signal an absent owner.
The door was closed, the garden untended, and dirt smeared the Windows.
He says that he wishes he could afford some steak, but it is better than leaving fertile ground untended.
The tests showed that, when untended by people, all four species of crop did badly.
But if the unkempt office plant in the corner is left untended it can soon turn into a health hazard.
